examples.title: Lamport Clock Implementation
examples.description: Pseudocode showing how each process maintains and updates Lamport timestamps
examples.explanation: Shows increment on local events, embedding timestamp in messages, and update rule on receive using max+1. Also shows how total ordering is achieved by delivering in timestamp order.

examples.title: Vector Clock Implementation
examples.description: Pseudocode showing vector clock maintenance and comparison operations
examples.explanation: Shows vector clock mechanics: increment own component, update all components on receive using max, and comparison rules to determine causality vs concurrency.

examples.title: Causal Order Broadcast
examples.description: Pseudocode implementing causal broadcast using vector clocks
examples.explanation: Shows how causal broadcast works: a message is only delivered when all causally preceding messages have been delivered. Uses vector clocks to track dependencies.

examples.title: Detecting Version Conflicts
examples.description: Pseudocode showing conflict detection in distributed data stores using vector clocks
examples.explanation: Shows practical application: detecting when concurrent edits conflict (neither version is ancestor of other) vs when one is an update to the other. Essential for eventually consistent systems.
